message:
fix(types,tls): Budget priority uses max (higher urgency wins) and CertificatePinSet secure-by-default

Budget (types/budget.rs): correct priority combination semantics from
min to max. In the Budget lattice, higher priority values represent
higher urgency, so the meet operation should preserve the most urgent
(highest) priority rather than the least urgent. Update doc comments
in both the module-level table and the combine/meet method docs.

CertificatePinSet (tls/types.rs): replace derive(Default) with a
manual Default impl that delegates to Self::new(), which sets
enforce=true. The derived Default set enforce=false (bool default),
creating an insecure-by-default pin set that would silently pass
validation even when pins don't match.

Formatting-only changes (no behavioral impact):
- asupersync.rs: expand single-line clippy allow list to multi-line
- tower_bridge.rs: rustfmt .await chain
- builder.rs: rustfmt map_or_else chain
- blocking_pool.rs: reorder std::sync imports alphabetically
- watch.rs: reorder std::sync imports alphabetically

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,5 +1,15 @@
 //! Asupersync CLI tools (feature-gated).
-#![allow(clippy::result_large_err, clippy::too_many_lines, clippy::too_many_arguments, clippy::format_push_string, clippy::case_sensitive_file_extension_comparisons, clippy::redundant_closure_for_method_calls, clippy::option_if_let_else, clippy::redundant_clone, clippy::uninlined_format_args)]
+#![allow(
+    clippy::result_large_err,
+    clippy::too_many_lines,

+    clippy::case_sensitive_file_extension_comparisons,
+    clippy::redundant_closure_for_method_calls,
+    clippy::option_if_let_else,

+    clippy::uninlined_format_args
+)]
